channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: White House looks to Republican-led states to crack down on immigration
item.description: President Trump points to Tennessee as a model for cooperating with the immigration crackdown but some in the state don't like what it means.
item.content:encoded: President Trump points to Tennessee as a model for cooperating with the immigration crackdown but some in the state don't like what it means.
item.dc:creator: Marianna Bacallao

item.title: Pakistan hosts historic face-to-face talks between the US and Iran
item.description: In Pakistan's capital, Islamabad, officials from the US and Iran met to discuss a possible end to the war that's engulfed much of the Middle East.
item.content:encoded: In Pakistan's capital, Islamabad, officials from the US and Iran met to discuss a possible end to the war that's engulfed much of the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Betsy Joles

item.title: Artemis II mission inspires the next generation of space scientists
item.description: Most of the world's population was not alive during the days of the Apollo moon program. What do young people make of this latest moonshot?
item.content:encoded: Most of the world's population was not alive during the days of the Apollo moon program. What do young people make of this latest moonshot?
item.dc:creator: Nell Greenfieldboyce

item.title: At a concert in Budapest, anti-Orbán sentiments take center stage ahead of election
item.description: At a concert in Budapest, musicians and concertgoers express criticism of Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's leadership.
item.content:encoded: At a concert in Budapest, musicians and concertgoers express criticism of Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's leadership.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: How Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's hometown became a symbol of excesses
item.description: Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán has long been accused of corruption. Sightseers now flock to his hometown as groups aim to raise awareness of what they say are the leader's excesses.
item.content:encoded:
Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán has long been accused of corruption. Sightseers now flock to his hometown as groups aim to raise awareness of what they say are the leader's excesses.

(Image credit: Rob Schmitz)
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: In Hungary, upcoming elections could bring an end to Orban's 16-year rule
item.description: In Hungary, voters head to the polls Sunday. At stake: the future for populist Prime Minister Viktor Orbán, Europe's longest-serving leader - and an ally of Presidents Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin.
item.content:encoded: In Hungary, voters head to the polls Sunday. At stake: the future for populist Prime Minister Viktor Orbán, Europe's longest-serving leader - and an ally of Presidents Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: What's it like to cover the Ukraine war as the world's attention is focused on Iran?
item.description: In this week's Reporter's Notebook conversation, we take a closer look at what it's like to cover the war in Ukraine, and how the war in Iran is changing that.
item.content:encoded: In this week's Reporter's Notebook conversation, we take a closer look at what it's like to cover the war in Ukraine, and how the war in Iran is changing that.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel J. Sánchez

item.title: Peru's election: A battle for the Presidency amid political chaos and crime
item.description: With 35 candidates in the race, Peru is set to elect its 9th president in less than a decade. Amid rising corruption and crime, voters are left asking: Can this election finally break the cycle?
item.content:encoded:
With 35 candidates in the race, Peru is set to elect its 9th president in less than a decade. Amid rising corruption and crime, voters are left asking: Can this election finally break the cycle?

(Image credit: Martin Mejia)
item.dc:creator: Simeon Tegel

item.title: From threats to civilization to a ceasefire: The week in Washington
item.description: President Trump made a dramatic shift on Iran this week as critics within his own party pushed back on the war.
item.content:encoded: President Trump made a dramatic shift on Iran this week as critics within his own party pushed back on the war.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro

item.title: At 86, Ian McKellen is still taking on new projects like 'The Christophers'
item.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Sir Ian McKellen about playing a sour painter in The Christophers and why the 86-year-old actor hopes to never retire.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Sir Ian McKellen about playing a sour painter in The Christophers and why the 86-year-old actor hopes to never retire.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Handel
